sourceMovie.title: Frenzy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Frenzy works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Last House on the Left
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Frenzy clicked for you, The Last House on the Left (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ocean's Eleven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Frenzy clicked for you, Ocean's Eleven (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Frenzy?
faq.answer: Try The Last House on the Left, Ocean's Eleven, Jackie Brown—each shares DNA with Frenzy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Frenzy?
faq.answer: Frenzy (1972) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Frenzy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
